key_facts: RBI is a Canadian corporation, sole general partner of RBILP
Four brands: Tim Hortons, Burger King, Popeyes, Firehouse Subs
Functional currency: Canadian dollar for TH Canada, US dollar for most other operations
Foreign currency gains/losses in other operating expenses

key_facts.cash_equivalents: Original maturities ≤3 months and credit card receivables
key_facts.lessor_accounting: Operating leases: straight-line over term; Sales-type and direct financing on net basis
key_facts.goodwill_impairment: Annual test Oct 1. Carrols BK reporting unit fair value ~7.0% above carrying value of $1,000M. Firehouse Brand/unit >20% above carrying
key_facts.long_lived_assets: Reviewed for impairment when events indicate
key_facts.derivatives: Carried at fair value using Level 2 inputs
key_facts.gift_card_breakage: Recognized proportionately as redeemed using historical breakage rate
key_facts.franchise_incentives: Cash/non-cash incentives capitalized and amortized as reduction in franchise/property revenue over contract term
key_facts.income_taxes: More-likely-than-not threshold; measured at largest amount >50% likely
key_facts.new_accounting_standards: Hedge Accounting Improvements (ASU, effective after Dec 2026); Income tax disclosure improvements

key_facts: Basic EPS 2025: $2.36; Diluted: $2.35
Weighted avg shares: Basic 329M, Diluted 457M
Six operating segments: TH Canada, TH International, BK US&Canada, BK International, PLK, FHS
BK China reported as discontinued operations from Q1 2025
CODM manages assets on consolidated basis — segment assets not disclosed
Long-lived assets by country disclosed
Unsatisfied performance obligations disclosed by year through 2030+
Acquisition date: May 16, 2024
Finalized acquisition accounting Q1 2025
Final allocation of consideration to net tangible and intangible assets presented
Prior to Feb 14, 2025: equity method investment in Pangaea Foods (China) Holdings Ltd.
Feb 14, 2025: acquired remaining equity for ~$151M cash
Classified as held for sale and discontinued operations
Assets held for sale: $489M
Net loss from discontinued operations: $126M
Cash used for discontinued operations disclosed
TIMWEN Partnership (50% JV with Wendy's): real estate under Canadian combination restaurants
Distributions received: $15M (2025), $14M (2024), $13M (2023)
Rent expense to TIMWEN: $21M each year
Intangible assets net: $11,190M (2025)
Goodwill: $6,306M (2025)
No impairment charges in 2025, 2024, 2023
Breakdown of current and non-current other liabilities
Term Loan B and Term Loan A facilities
Senior secured revolving credit facility
Senior Notes (multiple tranches)
Customary affirmative and negative covenants: limit indebtedness, liens, mergers, asset sales, dividends
Interest expense net detailed
Cross-currency rate swaps: $5,700M notional to hedge Canadian subsidiary net investment
Restructured $5,000M in Nov 2024 with maturities Sep 2028 - Oct 2030
No credit-risk contingent features
Special voting share for Partnership exchangeable unit holders
346,323,165 common shares outstanding at Dec 31, 2025
AOCI components: foreign currency translation, derivatives, pensions
RSU activity: time-vested and performance-based
~4,700 restaurant properties leased/subleased to franchisees
Initial terms: 10-20 years
Fixed monthly payments with escalations and renewal options
Property revenue: rental income from operating leases + earned income on direct financing leases
Future minimum lease receipts and commitments disclosed by year (2026-2030+)
Current and deferred tax expense by jurisdiction (Canadian, US Federal, US State, Foreign)
Canada federal statutory rate used as base
Rate reconciliation items: provincial taxes, foreign tax effects, GILTI
GILTI: elected to account as period cost
Deferred tax assets and liabilities detailed
Operating loss and tax credit carryforwards with expiration dates
Income tax payments by jurisdiction
IT/telecom services: $67M over 4 years
Advertising purchase commitments: $195M (mostly within 12 months)
Jan 6, 2026: Cash dividend $0.62 per share paid
Partnership distribution $0.62 per exchangeable unit
